data.title: DoJ asks judge to dismiss criminal charge over Boeing 737 MAX crashes
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Justice asked a judge Thursday to dismiss a criminal fraud charge against Boeing (NYSE:BA) related to two fatal 737 MAX plane crashes that killed 346 people, after reaching an agreement in principle with the company last week.

Under the deal terms, Boeing (NYSE:BA) will avoid being named a convicted felon but will pay an additional $444.5M into a fund for crash victims that would be divided evenly per crash victim on top of an additional $243.6M fine, pay $1.1B including the fine and compensation to families, and $455M to strengthen the company's compliance, safety, and quality programs, the DoJ said.

The agreement "secures meaningful accountability, delivers substantial and immediate public benefits, and brings finality to a difficult and complex case whose outcome would otherwise be uncertain," the DoJ said.

Victims' families have long maintained that Boeing (BA) and its executives should be held criminally accountable for design flaws tied to the 2018 and 2019 crashes.

data.title: Microchip raises guidance for Q1 sales and EPS
data.description:
• Microchip Technology (NASDAQ:MCHP) on Thursday said it now expects consolidated net sales for the June quarter to be between $1.045 billion and $1.070 billion.
  • Microchip previously provided guidance on May 8, 2025 of consolidated net sales to be between $1.025 billion and $1.070 billion.
  • The company sees Q1 GAAP loss per share between $(0.11) and $(0.07), while non-GAAP earnings per share is expected to be between $0.22 and $0.26.
  • The original guidance for the GAAP loss per share was $(0.15) and $(0.07), and the original guidance for non-GAAP earnings per share was between $0.18 and $0.26.
  • Consensus for Q1 sales and EPS are $1.05B and $0.22.
  • "Our bookings activity for the month of May is tracking to be higher than any month in the last two years. We are gaining confidence in the recovery of our business as we execute on our strategic initiatives, reduce inventory levels and make progress towards our long-term business model," said CEO Steve Sanghi.
  • MCHP +1.73% after hours to $59.12.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Rigetti Computing files automatic mixed securities shelf

data.title: Gap warns of incremental costs from tariffs, shares slide 17% after-hours
data.description:
Gap (NYSE:GAP) shares fell sharply in late Thursday trading as concerns over the company’s guidance—issued without accounting for potential tariff impacts—overshadowed an otherwise strong first-quarter performance.

“Gap Inc delivered strong first quarter results, exceeding financial expectations and gaining market share for the 9th consecutive quarter,” said the company’s CEO.

But despite warning that tariffs could negatively impact operating income by $100M to $150M including mitigation strategies, guidance for the full year does not include any impact to the company’s top-line.  

Additionally, the company’s expectation for net sales of $3.72B in the current quarter – which is flat from the same quarter last year – is below the consensus estimate of $3.73B.

For FY25, Gap (NYSE:GAP) expects net sales to increase by 1% to 2%, representing a range of $15.25B to $15.40B, straddling the $15.28B consensus estimates. Operating income is expected to increase by 8% to 10%, or a range of $1.19B to $1.21B.

For the reported quarter, Gap (NYSE:GAP) comparable sales rose 2% company-wide which incorporates a 3% increase in Old Navy, +5% for Gap banner stores, down 8% for Athleta and flat comparable sales in Banana Republic. Online sales were up 6%, all of which contributed to a 2% increase in net sales to $3.5B, $40M better than expected.

Earnings of $0.51 per share increased from $0.41 last year and came in 6 cents better than estimates. Gross margin expanded 60 basis points to 41.8%, while merchandise margin was flat from a year ago.

Gap (GAP) shares are down more than 17%.

data.title: Wells Fargo to sell assets of rail equipment leasing unit
data.description:
Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) announced on Thursday a definitive agreement to sell the assets of its rail equipment leasing business to a GATX Corporation-Brookfield Infrastructure joint venture.

The sale includes rail operating lease assets, with a book value of ~$4.4B, and the rail finance lease portfolio.

The transaction is expected to close in Q1 2026 or sooner.

The terms of the deal were not disclosed.

"This transaction is consistent with Wells Fargo's ongoing strategy of simplifying our businesses and focusing on products and services that are core to our clients," said David Marks, executive vice president at the commercial banking unit.

Wells Fargo said the sale is not expected to have a material impact on its financial position or earnings.

data.title: Labor market 'solid,' fight against inflation is making progress, SF Fed's Daly says
data.description:
"I really think the labor market is in solid shape," San Francisco Fed President Mary C. Daly said on Thursday in discussing the central bank's two mandates of full employment and price stability.

"It may take a little longer to find a job" than when the economy was "frothy," but workers are generally getting the jobs they want, she said.

As for the Fed's 2% inflation target, "I can't guarantee that we'll get there this year," she said, adding that it's not in her forecast.

"We are making progress," Daly said. "More importantly, we are resolute in getting the job done."

Note, though, that the Fed has no number to define full employment. At last check, the unemployment rate stood at 4.2% in April. As for inflation, the March PCE price index rose 2.6% Y/Y.

During many periods, the Fed's two mandates are not in conflict. But when they are, then "you have to balance the risks," she said. "You're not just balancing today, but over time" because monetary policy works with a lag.

When asked about Moody's downgrade of the U.S. sovereign credit rating, the Federal Reserve doesn't make fiscal policy, Daly said. Rather, it makes monetary policy. She declined to say whether the downgrade makes her job any harder. She's focused on stable prices and full employment.

Every new administration coming into the White has a list of policies they want to implement, Daly said. When a new administration comes in, she looks at three things:

  • How do the proposals get amended, changed or modified?
  • What is the end-state of the proposals? What is the scope magnitude, and timing of the end-stat?.
  • What's the net effect of the resulting government action?

Right now, businesses are pausing as they assess the effects of new policies, she said.  As a result, the Fed is in a wait-and-see model

"We're in a good place right now" in terms of monetary policy, she said. Note that the federal funds rate target range now sits at 4.25%-4.50%.

Independence of the Federal Reserve "is essential." Therefore, she doesn't make political comments. 

Discussing the attacks by the Trump administration on the Fed's independence, Daly said it's not the first time that the Federal Reserve has been asked by an administration to take certain actions. Criticism from elected officials "is part of the job," she said. However, it's important that the Fed doesn't let political considerations affect its monetary policy decisions.  

The Fed is accountable to the U.S. Congress, but also to the American people, she said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Bowen Acquisition receives NASDAQ’s notification of non-compliance
data.description:
• Bowen Acquisition Corp (NASDAQ:BOWN) said on Thursday that it has received a letter from the Nasdaq’s listing department advising the company that it does not comply with Nasdaq’s listing rule for continued listing because NASDAQ has not received the company’s quarterly report on Form 10-Q. 
  • NASDAQ has informed the company that it has until July 28, 2025, to submit a plan to regain compliance regarding this delinquent report. 
  • The company said it is working diligently to complete the Form 10-Q.

data.title: Femasys announces public offering, concurrent private placement
data.description:
• Femasys (NASDAQ:FEMY) plans to sell common stock in an underwritten public offering. A 30-day option will be granted to underwriters to purchase up to 15% additional shares.
  • All securities in both offerings will be sold by Femasys.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Allied Gaming & Entertainment receives expected Nasdaq notice regarding delayed quarterly report
data.description:
• Allied Gaming & Entertainment (NASDAQ:AGAE) on Thursday said it has received a deficiency letter from Nasdaq, notifying the company that it is not in compliance with the requirements for continued listing, as the company’s Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the quarter ended March 31, 2025 was not filed with the SEC by the required due date of May 15, 2025.
  • The company also remains delinquent in filing its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024.
  • Under Nasdaq rules, the company has until June 16, 2025, to submit a plan to regain compliance.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Rush Enterprises increases stock repurchase program to $200M
data.description:
• Rush Enterprises (NASDAQ:RUSHA) boosts its share repurchase program by $50M, raising the total authorization to $200M.
  • The increase follows the company nearing its original $150M buyback limit.
  • The program runs through December 31, 2025.
  • Source: Press release
